rationale: Nair et al. is cited not only in the literature review but also again in the model-development discussion to justify focusing on influencer-related effects and opinion leaders. The citation has a clear functional role: it provides prior empirical evidence that opinion leaders can influence peers, which helps position why influencer characteristics should matter in the citing paper's diffusion model. Although the citing study does not adopt Nair et al.'s exact empirical design, it uses that paper as meaningful conceptual and empirical grounding rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Nair et al. (2010) as prior empirical support that opinion leaders affect others' behavior and uses this to motivate its influencer-effects framing. It then builds a different diffusion model and context, but on that foundational premise that influential actors in networks matter.
